method: Read the 5x contact sheets tools/out/sheets/ruindungeons-r*-c24-48.png, then re-cropped every claimed region at 10-22x with a magenta tile grid and a magenta/blue transparency checker, plus a per-cell alpha histogram (fully-opaque / fully-transparent / mixed) computed straight from the PNG. Every coordinate below was LOOKED AT. Cells I could not read are marked unknown.

themes.look: bone-white / pale marble with steel-blue accents
terracotta / red-brown, jade + gold inlay, mesoamerican

themeStructureNote: The three themes are the SAME layout recolored + re-detailed. Column bands: c26-28 = water autotile; c32-39 = props (plaques, urns, rubble, statues); c40-43 = building set (frame -> pilaster+gable -> brick wall); c44-46 = second wall material (planks, then stone masonry); c47 = shelves + ladder. Rows 0, 17, 34, 51 are blank separator rows across c24-48.

blockCandidates.name: pale_temple_brick
blockCandidates.why: Alpha-verified 256/256 opaque. Plain bone-white ashlar brick with grey-green mortar, no trim, no gable diagonal. The clean fill row of the pale building set (r14 has the pediment diagonal, r16 adds a blue basecourse stripe). Best ruined-temple / ossuary wall we have.

blockCandidates.name: pale_temple_brick_b
blockCandidates.why: Identical partner tile to (41,15) with a different brick offset - use the pair to break repetition on long walls. Fully opaque.

blockCandidates.name: pale_ruined_masonry
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque. Centre column of the 3x2 pale rubble-masonry panel (c44-46 x r14-15); c44/c46 carry vertical pilaster trim so ONLY c45 is safe. Irregular cracked stones with blue-grey gaps - reads as crumbling crypt wall / ossuary fill.

blockCandidates.name: pale_plank_boards
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque. Centre of the 3x2 cream+blue horizontal-board panel (c44-46 x r11-12); c44/c46 have edge posts. Painted plank boarding - prison hoarding / sewer boardwalk face. (45,11) is the same tile with a grey grime smear (variant).

blockCandidates.name: pale_fluted_column
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque, vertically fluted marble pilaster with a dark seam down one edge - tiles vertically into a column shaft. Caveat: it is the LEFT pilaster of the building set, so the seam is on one side only (c43 is its mirror).

blockCandidates.name: sandstone_tomb_brick
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque. Plain gold-sandstone ashlar, the clean fill row of the gold building set (r31 carries the roof diagonal, r33 a cream basecourse). Tomb / pharaoh-vault wall.

blockCandidates.name: sandstone_tomb_brick_b
blockCandidates.why: Opaque partner tile of (41,32), different brick offset.

blockCandidates.name: dungeon_stone_brick
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque. Centre of the brown stacked-stone masonry panel (c44-46 x r31-32); c44/c46 have gold edge posts. Warm brown irregular blocks - generic dungeon / catacomb masonry.

blockCandidates.name: wood_planks_brown
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque. Centre of the brown horizontal plank panel (c44-46 x r28-29). Clean board texture, no nails/edges - good prison-floor / boarded-passage block.

blockCandidates.name: sandstone_fluted_column
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque tan fluted pilaster tile, gold-theme twin of (40,11). Same one-sided-seam caveat.

blockCandidates.name: dark_red_brick
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque. Plain dark maroon/red brick, clean fill row of the terracotta building set (r48 has the roof diagonal + gold trim, r50 a gold basecourse). The darkest masonry on the sheet - sewer / catacomb / blood-temple mood we currently lack.

blockCandidates.name: ruin_cobble_brown
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque. Centre of the brown rough-cobble panel (c44-46 x r48-49); outer columns carry gold posts. Rougher, more irregular than (45,32) - collapsed-ruin fill.

blockCandidates.name: wood_planks_brown_b
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque; terracotta-theme twin of (45,29), very slightly warmer boards. Centre of c44-46 x r45-46.

blockCandidates.name: clay_fluted_column
blockCandidates.why: Fully opaque terracotta fluted pilaster, clay-theme twin of (40,11).

blockCandidates.name: pale_pool_water
blockCandidates.why: AUTOTILE CENTRE of the 3x3 outer block c26-28 x r3-5 (pale-marble pool rim). Pure animated water, fully opaque - this is the ONLY safe cell of that blob. Frames 2 and 3 are (27,9) and (27,14).

blockCandidates.name: sandstone_pool_water
blockCandidates.why: AUTOTILE CENTRE of the 3x3 block c26-28 x r20-22 (sandstone rim). Frames 2/3 at (27,26) and (27,31).

blockCandidates.name: clay_pool_water
blockCandidates.why: AUTOTILE CENTRE of the 3x3 block c26-28 x r37-39 (terracotta rim). Frames 2/3 at (27,43) and (27,48).

rejectedButOpaque.note: Same brick as the fill row but with a coloured basecourse stripe along the bottom. Fine as a plinth/trim row, wrong as a cube face.
Brick wall with a pilaster edge stripe baked in. Corner pieces of the building set.
Carry vertical edge posts/trim. Only the c45 centre column is a clean tile.
Opaque, but it is the middle of the big ornate fountain object (c45-47 x r4-6).
Opaque, but they are the underside slabs of the gold biers.
Opaque, but interior cells of the 4-wide gold sarcophagi.
Opaque, but interior cells of the giant stone/jade heads.

autotileRegions.kind: RPG-Maker A1 water autotile, PALE MARBLE rim, 3 animation frames
autotileRegions.layout: Each frame = 2 rows of inner-corner pieces (c27-28 only; c26 blank) followed by a 3x3 outer block (c26-28). Frame 1: inner r1-2, block r3-5. Frame 2: inner r6-7, block r8-10. Frame 3: inner r11-12, block r13-15.
autotileRegions.safeCells: (27,4), (27,9), (27,14) - the 3x3 centres = pure water. EVERY other cell has a rim/notch.

autotileRegions.kind: same water autotile, SANDSTONE rim, 3 frames
autotileRegions.layout: Frame 1: inner r18-19, block r20-22. Frame 2: inner r23-24, block r25-27. Frame 3: inner r28-29, block r30-32.

autotileRegions.kind: same water autotile, TERRACOTTA rim, 3 frames
autotileRegions.layout: Frame 1: inner r35-36, block r37-39. Frame 2: inner r40-41, block r42-44. Frame 3: inner r45-46, block r47-49.

autotileRegions.kind: NOT an autotile but an ASSEMBLED BUILDING ELEVATION - treat it the same way
autotileRegions.layout: rows +0..+1 = ornate arch/window frame (transparent interior); rows +2..+6 = fluted pilasters at c40 & c43 with a gable/pediment filling c41-c42; rows +7..+8 = the brick wall (row +7 plain, row +8 with basecourse). Grabbing anything from the pilaster/gable band ships a diagonal roof edge or a column seam.

autotileRegions.kind: two 3x2 wall-material panels stacked (planks on top, stone masonry below), separated by a blank row
autotileRegions.layout: Pale: planks r11-12, blank r13, rubble masonry r14-15. Gold: planks r28-29, blank r30, stone brick r31-32. Clay: planks r45-46, blank r47, cobble r48-49. Left column (c44) and right column (c46) both carry a vertical edge post - only c45 is a clean tile.

Pale (r1-2): four stone basins side by side at c40-41, c42-43, c44-45, c46-47; the right two are overgrown with green vines. Gold (r18-19): two 4x2 gold-rimmed open coffers filled with grey stone rubble.

emptyCells.columns: c24, c25, c29, c30, c31 and c48 are blank across essentially the whole sheet in this half (c24 has only three 2-cell fragments at r9-10, r24-25, r43-44 that belong to objects in the c0-23 half).
emptyCells.rows: r0, r17, r34, r51 are fully blank in c24-48 (theme separators).

moodsGained: crypt / ossuary: pale_temple_brick + pale_ruined_masonry + the moss/vine set (32-39, 11-16) + skull-frieze altar (32-34, 45-47)
ruined temple: fallen columns (40-46, 3), balustrade (40-44, 4-6), fountain (45-47, 4-6), rune steles
sewer / catacomb: dark_red_brick + ruin_cobble_brown + wood_planks_brown + floor stains (44-47, 7/24/41) + drips (40-43, 7/24/41)
tomb: sandstone_tomb_brick + sarcophagi + mummy cases + canopic jars + Anubis statues
ancient / eldritch: jade heads (40-47, 37-38), tiki idols (32-39, 48-50), glowing red gems (32-39, 41), brazier (37-38, 50)
prison: red-mattress shackle slabs (32-39, 30-31), iron-less but boarded plank walls, ladders
traps: Every c44/c46 cell of a c44-46 panel has a vertical trim post baked in. Only c45 is a clean tile.
The building-set brick wall is only TWO rows tall per theme (pale r15-16, gold r32-33, clay r49-50) and the lower of the two carries a coloured basecourse stripe. Use the upper row.
The three water blobs are true RPG-Maker autotiles with THREE animation frames stacked vertically. Only the 3x3 centres are tileable.
The rows just above each brick wall look like brick but contain the pediment diagonal (pale r14, gold r31, clay r48).
c47 is a narrow column of shelves/steps/ladders, never a full tile.
